data.title: Texas Roadhouse should see relief from beef inflation in 2026 — analyst
data.description:
With its share price trajectory in 2025 largely undermined by inflated beef prices, Texas Roadhouse (TXRH) can continue to capitalize on its quality service, strengthening value, and disciplined cost management that can give investors comfort that the “negative earnings revision cycle that plagued 2025 is over.”

“We admire Texas Roadhouse’s secret sauce of an intense focus on operations to ensure a great guest experience to drive repeat visits. While this strategy largely lacks tangible sales drivers, we expect a sustaining of the best comp track record within full service,” said TD Cowen analysts team led by Andrew Charles, initiating the restaurant chain with a Buy rating and $215 price target.

As it accounts for 50% of TXRH’s cost of goods sold, the central investor debate remains on beef prices, which became more problematic last year.

Charles and team, however, believe peak beef prices are in the past, down ~10% from the September highs, and should continue to moderate through 2026 and more meaningfully in 2027. This thesis is supported by evidence of demand destruction in grocery for steak, suggesting pent-up demand, which TXHR can benefit from as the “perceived value leader in the steakhouse category.”

Moreover, lower beef prices and expanded margins are supported by multiple factors, including regulatory scrutiny of meatpackers, low U.S. cattle inventories, tariff exemptions, easing rancher costs, and improved drought conditions.

“Beef inflation is the largest wild card, though we believe incremental news flow is more likely to be positive than negative…driven by beef prices that are off the highs with live cattle futures in backwardation,” Charles and team said in their note to clients.

And while the near term will be challenging, “it’s reasonable to assume margins will recover in 2027,” agrees Seeking Alpha analyst YR Research, who believes the long-term outlook for TXRH is “compelling” as margin recovery at 23% EPS growth are expected by 2027 as commodity pressures normalize.

Over the past year, shares have struggled to keep up with the S&P 500 but have already gained 17% in 2026 versus a 1.4% increase in the S&P 500, trading higher for 9 of the past 11 days.

data.title: Lindblad Expeditions to mandatorily convert Series A preferred stock
data.description:
• Lindblad Expeditions Holdings (NASDAQ: LIND) said on Tuesday it has exercised its right to mandatorily convert all outstanding shares of its 6.0% Series A convertible preferred stock into common stock.
  • The company said the conversion right was triggered on January 16 after its common shares traded above $14.25 on a volume-weighted average basis for at least 20 out of 30 consecutive trading days.
  • The conversion will become effective on February 3.
  • All 62,000 preferred shares will be converted into about 9.0 million common shares.
  • Following the conversion, Lindblad expects to have roughly 64.4 million common shares outstanding, with no preferred stock remaining.
  • LIND -0.55% premarket to $16.14.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Strategy buys bitcoins worth $2.13B in the week ended January 19
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) on Tuesday announced bitcoin acquisitions worth ~$2.13B during the period between January 12, 2026, and January 19, 2026.

The bitcoin treasury company purchased 22,305 tokens for an average price of $95,284.

This compares with 13,627 tokens bought for $1.25B at an average price of $91,519 apiece in the week before.

Shares were -4.81% pre-market to $165.35. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was trading 1.80% lower at $90,915.82 on Tuesday before the U.S. market open.

Last week's purchases bring the company's aggregate BTC holdings to 709,715, bought for $53.92B and an average price of $75,979.

The acquisitions were made using proceeds from Strategy's at-the-market offerings.

The Tysons Corner-based company sold 2,945,371 shares of Strategy Inc 9.0% SERIES A PERPETUAL STRETCH PREF STK (STRC) for net proceeds of $294.3M; 38,796 shares of Strategy Inc 8.00% SERIES A PERPETUAL STRIKE PFD (STRK) for $3.4M; and 10,399,650 class A shares of common stock for $1.83B.

data.title: Sankar Das Gupta exercises warrants to acquire Electrovaya's common shares
data.description:
• Electrovaya (ELVA) announced on Tuesday that Sankar Das Gupta, a director and chairman of the board of directors of Electrovaya, exercised 1,420,000 warrants to purchase common shares at $0.90 per common share, acquiring an equivalent number of common shares on the exercise.
  • Immediately before the exercise, Sankar Das Gupta owned 10,270,751 common shares on a non-diluted basis, representing approximately 21.40% of the outstanding common shares, 605,000 options entitling Gupta to purchase an additional 605,000 common shares, and 1,420,000 warrants entitling the acquirer to purchase an additional 1,420,000 common shares. 
  • Following the exercise, the acquirer owns 11,690,751 common shares on a non-diluted basis, representing approximately 23.66% of the outstanding common shares, and 605,000 options to purchase common shares, entitling the acquirer to purchase an additional 605,000 common shares. 
  • ELVA is down 3.05% premarket to $9.52.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Acadia Healthcare appoints Debbie Osteen as CEO; EPS outlook trails estimates
data.description:
• Acadia Healthcare Company (ACHC) Tuesday said that Debra K. Osteen, former Board member and former chief executive officer of Acadia, has been appointed CEO, effective immediately.
  • Osteen, who has also been appointed as a member of the Board, succeeds Chris Hunter, who is departing the company.
  • The company also reaffirmed its 2025 outlook, projecting revenue of $3.28–$3.30 billion, in line with estimates of $3.29 billion; adjusted EBITDA of $601–$611 million; and adjusted earnings per share of $1.94–$2.04, compared with a consensus estimate of $2.05.

data.title: Rigetti secures $8.4M order for 108-qubit quantum computer
data.description:
• Rigetti Computing (RGTI) has received an $8.4M purchase order to deliver a 108-qubit quantum computer to the Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), India’s premier R&D organization of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology.
  • The system, which includes Rigetti’s proprietary chiplet-based architecture, is scheduled to be deployed on-premises at C-DAC’s Bengaluru center in the second half of 2026.
  • RGTI --3.4% premarket to $24.74
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nvidia may unveil Windows on Arm notebook this year: report
data.description:
• Nvidia (NVDA) could show off a new notebook PC that runs Microsoft's (MSFT) Windows later this year, Digitimes reported. 
  • The laptops may run Nvidia's N1 and N1X system-on-a-chip and may launch as soon as the first quarter, the news outlet added, citing its sources. The company has also reportedly shown off a product roadmap that runs through N2 and N2X platforms, the news outlet added.
  • Plans for Nvidia and AMD (AMD) to sell PC chips using architecture from Arm Holdings (ARM) have been in the works for a while, according to previous media reports. AMD already makes processors for desktops and notebooks using x86 architecture, which is owned by Intel (INTC).
  • Nvidia did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Late last year, Arm and Nvidia expanded their partnership to integrate Nvidia's NVLink Fusion ecosystem into its Neoverse platform. Nvidia's NVLink Fusion connects CPUs and GPUs at a scale to run AI applications.
  • Nvidia shares fell 2% in premarket trading on Tuesday, while Arm fell 2.6%.

data.title: Acadia Healthcare names Debra Osteen as CEO; reaffirms guidance
data.description:
• Acadia Healthcare (ACHC) said Debra Osteen, former board member and former chief executive officer, has been appointed CEO, effective immediately.
  • Osteen, who has also been appointed as a member of the board, succeeds Chris Hunter, who is departing the company and its board.
  • Separately, Acadia reaffirmed its 2025 financial guidance for revenue of $3.28B-$3.30B (vs. $3.29B estimated), adjusted EBITDA of $601M-$611M, and adjusted earnings per share of $1.94 to $2.04 (vs. $2.05 estimated).
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PayPal offers free DIY tax filing for U.S. debit card customers
data.description:
PayPal Holdings (PYPL) partnered with april, a tax technology platform, to provide a free DIY tax filing service to its U.S. PayPal Debit Mastercard (MA) customers for 2025 federal and state taxes, the company said on Tuesday.

The offering saves customers ~$160 in typical costs, the company said.

PayPal (PYPL) stock dropped 1.8% in premarket trading.

The debit card customers can file taxes following a step-by-step flow through april's tax engine to enter information, upload documents, and file electronically with the IRS. For assistance, customers can use april's AI-powered chatbot or obtain live support for a fee. The average time to file is less than 20 minutes, PayPal said.

"Our partnership with april empowers customers to manage even more of their finances in one trusted place, helping them streamline tax preparation and take meaningful steps toward setting up their finances for the year ahead," said Shanthi Sarkar, VP of Debit and Money Management at PayPal (PYPL).

data.title: Rio Tinto brings 25-MW solar plant online at Kennecott mine
data.description:
• Rio Tinto (RIO) has brought a new 25-megawatt solar plant online at its Kennecott copper operations in Utah, using tellurium produced at the site to make the solar panels that now help power the facility, the company said Tuesday.
  • Together with the 5MW solar plant completed in 2023, Kennecott now has 30MW of solar capacity—enough to power approximately 1,026 average American homes annually and reduce Kennecott’s Scope 2 emissions by about 6% (20,000 tonnes CO₂e). 
  • Construction of the 25MW plant began in October 2024 in partnership with Bechtel, was completed and commissioned in October last year, and energized in December. The new solar array includes over 71,000 panels, containing tellurium produced at Kennecott, a critical mineral for solar technology.
  • Kennecott began producing tellurium in 2022 as a byproduct of copper refining, making it one of only two U.S. producers of this critical mineral.
